title: dactylorhiza × braunii nothovar. alpina
intro: Dactylorhiza × braunii nothovar. alpina, also known as the Braun's orchid, is a hybrid orchid species that belongs to the Dactylorhiza genus, resulting from the cross between Dactylorhiza maculata and Dactylorhiza sambucina. The Braun's orchid is valued for its medicinal properties, as it contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. This plant is said to offer various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antiseptic and antiviral agent. The Braun's orchid is typically used in herbal preparations such as tinctures, infusions, and salves, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional due to potential interactions with medications or allergies.
